texto: What I am afraid of is the first thing I was ever aware of being afraid of and what I have told my daughter countless times she need not fear: being alone in the dark. It is a small prison of emotion from which there is no escape. It is also, in its own way, a shattering revelation.
explicacao: Orientação direta de S. C. Gwynne sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “What I am afraid of is the first thing I was ever aware of being afraid of and what I have told m…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, fear, daughter, útil em momentos de reflexao-noturna e daughter.

It is a small prison of emotion from which there is no escape. It is also, in its own way, a shattering revelation.

texto: To make matters worse, Jackson placed great value on regurgitating every last detail of the assigned texts. When, in response to Jackson's question 'What are the three simple machines?' a cadet answered, 'The inclined plane, the lever, and the wheel,' Jackson replied, 'No, sir. The lever, the wheel, and the inclined plane.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de S.C. Gwynne que provoca temas ligados a humor. Ao questionar “To make matters worse, Jackson placed great value on regurgitating every last detail of the assig…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, civil-war, history, com foco em history e humor.
